tool_catalog: Call MCP tools/list to obtain the canonical tool catalogue with descriptions and JSON Schemas. There are 8 tools in v0.1.

envelope.success: Tool-specific JSON object. Each tool's description names its return keys in a RETURNS line.

error.rule: Agents MUST branch on `code`. `hint` is free-form and may name involved identifiers; do not parse it. `retryable=true` means the same call may succeed later without an agent-side state change.

error_vocabulary.semantics: Referenced offer_id is not in the ledger.
Referenced proposal_id is not in the ledger.
Referenced deal_id has no row in closed_deals.
The proposal already has an ACCEPT or REJECT; further mutation is rejected.
Caller is not in the allowed-callers set for that message. The hint names caller and allowed identities.
The MCP tool name is not registered.

idempotency.behavior: Two calls with the same scope produce the same effect. The second call returns the first call's result with status='duplicate'. Without idempotency_key, retries may create duplicates.
idempotency.key_constraints: string up to 100 chars; client-supplied; opaque to the server.

authorization.allowed: any agent; caller becomes seller of the new offer
any agent except the seller of the offer
seller of the underlying offer or buyer that originated the proposal
only the seller of the offer underlying the proposal
seller of the offer or buyer that originated the proposal
seller or buyer of the closed deal

invariants: Append-only ledger: protocol_messages rows are never updated or deleted; the full market state is reconstructable by replay.
Single ACCEPT closes the entire offer regardless of proposed_quantity; partial fulfilment is v0.2.
DISCOVER calls are persisted in the ledger as a search audit (with idempotency).
Identifiers (offer_id, proposal_id, counter_id, deal_id) are server-generated 12-char UUIDv4 prefixes; clients never invent them.
limitations_v0_1: No partial-quantity matching: one ACCEPT closes the offer.
No escrow: protocol records the agreement, does not move funds.
Geolocation is text only: location filter is ILIKE substring; location_radius_km / location_center are reserved for v0.2.
Reputation is trivial: counts deals, ignores disputes, ratings, and recency.
Counter chains are flat: COUNTER edits a proposal but the engine does not visualise threads.
v0.1 trusts the agent_id declared by the client; identity verification is required before exposing a public REST API.
